headline: The social measures tech leaders put forward to address AI's destructive potential sound familiar in Europe
description: Aware of the potentially devastating impact their technology could have on jobs and society, the leaders of OpenAI and Anthropic are advancing a range of progressive ideas aimed at preserving social cohesion.

Kenya and France strengthen their strategic partnership
description: Nairobi will host the 'Africa Forward' summit on May 11 and 12, co-organized with Paris. In recent years, the two countries have steadily strengthened their cooperation.

headline: Barcelona takes La Liga title after beating Real Madrid
description: Barcelona won their 29th Spanish league title after beating Madrid 2-0 on Sunday.

headline: How much space should cities give to wild vegetation?
description: Bordeaux's new city council has launched a major program to weed sidewalks across the city, while in Montpellier, officials are considering expanding what it calls France's first 'wild district.'

headline: 'The word 'masculinism' is deeply inappropriate and depoliticizes feminism'
description: Philosopher Geneviève Fraisse challenges, in an interview with Le Monde, certain words used to discuss issues related to the status of women that have spread through public discourse, in both activist circles and everyday language.

Pedro Almodóvar still burns with desire for cinema
description: Born during Madrid's cultural awakening, his work is celebrated around the world. But, despite several appearances at the Cannes Film Festival, Pedro Almodóvar has never won the Palme d'Or. Now 76 years old, the pioneer of Spanish auteur cinema is back in competition with 'Bitter Christmas,' in which he delves into the labyrinth of creation.

headline: How chicken is taking over France's fast-food market
description: The number of fast-food chains dedicated to chicken is booming in France. At the heart of this success is affordable comfort food, offering an unbeatable quantity-for-price ratio at a time when purchasing power is on the decline.

headline: Military deterrence has fully entered 'third nuclear age'
description: India, Pakistan, Ukraine, North Korea, Iran: The military revolution has also impacted the nuclear weapon debate, notably with the end of transparency and new announcements of increases to global arsenals, including in France.
